success_surface: Cell-Level Manipulation with Combined Data Transformation and Visual Styling
effective_behaviors: Selected openpyxl library for tasks requiring both cell-level data manipulation and formatting control
Respected precise row/column boundaries from answer_position specification when iterating
Applied multiple style properties (font, fill) to target cells within same processing loop for consistency
Handled potential None/null values gracefully during type conversion operations
Preserved original workbook by saving output to separate file path
Provided structured logging output documenting range processed, transformations applied, and output location
evidence: Used load_workbook() to access sheet, then ws.cell() for row-by-row iteration (range 2-17) matching G2:G16 specification
Applied both bold_font and fill_color PatternFill to each processed cell in column G
Checked 'if v is not None' before appending values for sorting and concatenation
Saved to output_path instead of overwriting input_path
Printed summary showing Range, Values transformation, Formatting, and Output saved locations

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL for Data + Style Tasks
memory_items.lesson: When a task requires both cell value manipulation and visual styling, use openpyxl directly rather than pandas alone
memory_items.generalization: Select openpyxl for cell-level tasks involving combined data transformation and format application; use pandas only for heavy data processing without formatting needs

memory_items.title: Boundary-Aware Row Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Extract exact row/column ranges from task specifications and iterate precisely within those bounds
memory_items.generalization: Parse answer_position or task constraints to determine exact iteration ranges, avoiding off-by-one errors or unnecessary processing outside target scope

memory_items.title: Unified Cell Styling Application
memory_items.lesson: Apply multiple formatting attributes to target cells within the same processing pass to ensure style-value consistency
memory_items.generalization: When writing values and applying styles, combine both operations on the same cell object before moving to next iteration

memory_items.title: Null Value Pre-Processing
memory_items.lesson: Filter out None/null values before type conversion or numeric operations to prevent runtime errors
memory_items.generalization: Always check for empty/null content before converting cell values to numeric types or performing calculations

memory_items.title: Source File Protection
memory_items.lesson: Never overwrite the input file; always write results to a new output path
memory_items.generalization: Preserve data integrity by saving modified workbooks to designated output paths separate from source locations

memory_items.title: Structured Execution Logging
memory_items.lesson: Document key execution details after completion for verification and debugging
memory_items.generalization: Include summary logs reporting affected ranges, transformation logic, styling applied, and final output location

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Combined Data + Styling**: When a task requires both cell value transformation and visual formatting, use `openpyxl` to apply styles immediately after writing values within the same loop iteration.
- **Boundary Precision**: Parse task specifications (like `answer_position`) to determine exact row/column ranges; iterate precisely within these bounds using `range(start, end + 1)` for inclusive Excel row numbers.
- **Null-Safe Conversion**: Always validate cell values are not None/empty before numeric type conversions (`int()`, `float()`).
- **Output Integrity**: Save all modifications to a new output file path; never overwrite the original workbook.
- **Execution Transparency**: After completion, log summary information including processed range, transformation logic, formatting applied, and output file location.
